issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus
issuesFound.explanation: The default browser focus indicator is removed for all elements that are not currently matching :focus-visible. If a user is using a browser or input method where :focus-visible is not triggered but :focus is, they will have no visual indication of which element has keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: /* Remove or provide a visible fallback for :focus */ :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px, which makes the base text size for the document significantly smaller than the standard 16px (1rem) recommendation. This forces users with visual impairments to zoom the page immediately to read content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting a negative word-spacing value causes words to be crowded together, which significantly degrades readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 195: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting a negative letter-spacing value reduces the whitespace between individual characters, making it harder for users to distinguish between letters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 196: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses "position: static", "width: auto", and "clip: auto", which fails to visually hide the element. Furthermore, the .not-sr-only class (Line 338) and the .visually-hidden-heading class (Line 445) use properties that do not correctly hide content from sighted users while keeping it for screen readers, or they use "position: absolute" without dimensions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 311: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view. There is no override within a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transition for the 'transform' property, which triggers movement on hover or state changes. This motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform transition to move the card 2px upwards on hover. While there is a transform: none rule on line 697, the transition itself is still active, which can cause stuttering or unwanted motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 697: .feature-card:hover { transform: none; transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" link arrow transitions its transform property on hover. This spatial movement is not guarded by a reduced motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 741: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards transition the transform property on hover. This spatial movement is not disabled in the provided reduced-motion block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards transition the transform property on hover. This spatial movement is not disabled in the provided reduced-motion block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and manage scroll locking but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation drawer has changed state.

issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module correctly manages `aria-describedby` and hidden attributes for error messages but fails to set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid="true"` to signal that a field requires correction.

issuesFound.title: click/mouse handlers on non-native elements without matching keyboard
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` module attaches `mouseenter` and `mouseleave` listeners to a wrapper element to open/close dropdowns. While it has keyboard handlers for the trigger, the hover-based interaction has no keyboard equivalent for users who do not move focus into the trigger (e.g., users navigating by touch-hover or specific assistive tools).

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus style like box-shadow or border. This removes focus indicators for all interactive elements when navigating via keyboard, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets properties like position: static and overflow: visible, which do not hide content visually. issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus-visible` removes the default outline (`outline: none`) without providing any visible replacement (no box‑shadow, border, or custom outline). Keyboard‑only users lose a clear focus indicator, violating SC 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` and only changes `outline-offset`, providing no visible focus styling for button components, which blocks keyboard navigation feedback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons lose their outline on `:focus-visible` without any replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which tab is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility incorrectly implemented (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto;` etc., which does **not** hide content from visual layout while keeping it accessible. This defeats its purpose and may cause screen‑reader users to miss hidden labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` on hover (rotate/scale) with a transition, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block for this selector is empty, so users who prefer reduced motion still experience the animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override:
  Line 460: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without alternative

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function opens or closes the mobile menu but never updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Assistive technologies cannot know the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button is not given an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` value, so screen readers assume an undefined state on page load.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open handler does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` makes the panel visible but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the associated button, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close handler does not set aria‑expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` hides the panel but does not reset `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving the state stale for assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation:
After registering dropdowns, the trigger buttons are not given an explicit `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, so the initial state is ambiguous.  
Fix (add to `register()` after creating `inst`):  
```javascript
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

---

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons do not update aria‑pressed when selected
issuesFound.explanation:
`productFilter._syncTabAriaStates()` toggles CSS classes but never updates the required `aria‑pressed` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which tab is active.  
Fix (inside the loop that sets `isActive`):  
```javascript
toggleAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));
```

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed on selection
issuesFound.explanation:
In `pricing._syncToggleState()` the period buttons receive a visual “active” class but their `aria‑pressed` state is never changed, violating the ARIA button pattern.  
Fix (inside the loop that iterates `_periodBtns`):  
```javascript
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));
```

---

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch does not maintain aria‑pressed state

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer, but the script never toggles the button’s `aria‑expanded` state. Assistive technologies therefore cannot tell whether the menu is currently visible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`), the script adds/removes CSS classes and hidden attributes but never reflects the state on the trigger button via `aria‑expanded`. Screen readers cannot perceive the open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab buttons do not reflect active state with aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Product‑filter tabs are styled as toggle buttons (`[data-filter-value]`). The code only toggles a CSS class (`CSS.active`) but does not set `aria‑pressed`, leaving the accessibility state ambiguous for keyboard and screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button does not expose its expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is currently open or closed, violating ARIA state management (WCAG 2.2 A).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are given `aria-controls` and `role="button"` but the trigger buttons never receive an `aria‑expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which answer is open.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not expose its open/closed state
issuesFound.explanation: The search box is given `role="combobox"` and `aria‑haspopup="listbox"` but never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute. Users of assistive technology cannot know whether the suggestion list is visible.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not expose pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The element that switches between monthly and annual pricing (`_toggleEl`) is used as a toggle but never receives `aria‑pressed`, so its state is invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: “Scroll‑to‑top” button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button that appears after scrolling does not have an `aria-label` or visible text, leaving screen‑reader users without a description of its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Form‑validation errors are not reflected with `aria‑invalid`
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markInvalid()` only adds `aria‑describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the invalid field. AT therefore cannot announce the error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline‑validation does not clear `aria‑invalid` on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, `srUtils.markValid()` removes the error message but leaves `aria‑invalid` untouched, causing a stale invalid state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons are not given `aria‑expanded` on initial load (duplicate of Issue 2 but for the initial DOM state)
issuesFound.explanation: Even before any interaction, the trigger should have `aria‑expanded="false"` so that AT knows the default state.
issuesFound.fix:
Same as Issue 2 – initialise the attribute to “false” in `register()`.

issuesFound.title: Missing page title

issuesFound.fix: Line 920: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 28px; }` sets a touch target height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate dropdown menu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `#site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 28px; height: 28px; }` sets both width and height to 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px and recommended 44px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate the search submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 970: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 28px; }` sets a touch target height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate filter tab buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1640: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 28px; }` sets a touch target height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate pricing toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 28px; }` sets a touch target height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate product CTA buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1780: `.product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The rules `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }` and `.hero-image img:hover { transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg); }` apply motion animations without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort or distraction.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.feature-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); }` applies a motion animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a:hover::after
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.feature-card a:hover::after { transform: translateX(3px); }` applies a motion animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.product-card:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); }` applies a motion animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.pricing-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); }` applies a motion animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); }` applies a motion animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `.stat-item:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); }` applies a motion animation without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `body { word-spacing: -0.2em; }` applies negative word-spacing, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by making text harder to distinguish.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `body { letter-spacing: -0.05em; }` applies negative letter-spacing, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by reducing character distinction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `body { letter-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) because users with colour blindness or low vision may not perceive the difference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 270: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative visual indicator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom) on hover/focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative on the base `:focus` state. Users who rely on keyboard navigation will not see a focus indicator on interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 398: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
    Line 401: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 612: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn-icon:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 650: `.btn-icon:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 702: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule removes the focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see a focus indicator on navigation links and buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule removes the focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see a focus indicator on dropdown menu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule removes the focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see a focus indicator on the search submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 770: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in the `mobileNav` module toggles the CSS class `is-open` on the navigation drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware of the navigation menu's state change, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button when the navigation drawer is closed on page load. Screen readers may misreport the initial state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in the `pricing` module updates the displayed prices and billing period labels but does not announce these changes to screen readers via an ARIA live region. Users relying on screen readers will not be aware of the billing period change, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validate` function in the `inlineValidator` module marks fields as invalid by adding an error class and showing an error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid fields. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification).

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validate` function in the `inlineValidator` module clears the error state by removing the error class and hiding the error message but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute on valid fields. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function in the `pricing` module updates the active state of billing period buttons but does not set `aria-pressed` on these buttons. Screen readers will not announce the selected billing period, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `pricing` module does not set an initial `aria-pressed` attribute on billing period buttons based on the initial period. Screen readers may misreport the initial state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the `is-open` class and update the DOM but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the navigation toggle button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, leaving keyboard and assistive technology users unaware of the menu's open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10123: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
    Line 10130: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-pressed on toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the `pricing` module toggles the billing period between monthly and annual but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the button's pressed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12150: `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(next === 'year'));` (insert after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`)

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the `pricing` module changes the billing period and updates the price display but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who rely on assistive technologies are not informed of the updated billing period, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 12150: `liveRegion.announce(next === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` (insert after `setPeriod(next);`)

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateAll` function in the `contactForm` module identifies invalid fields and renders error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid fields. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers cannot programmatically identify which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 13200: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (insert inside the `errors.push` block after `if (result && !result.valid) {`)

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module clears error messages and removes error classes when a field becomes valid but does not reset `aria-invalid` to `false` or remove the attribute. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 as the field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 13150: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` (insert after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on the navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) to reflect the initial closed state. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state is not programmatically exposed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10100: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (insert after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`)

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `pricing` module initialises the billing period toggle but does not set the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`) to reflect the initial state. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the pressed state is not programmatically exposed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12100: `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(initPeriod === 'year'));` (insert after `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);`)

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) in the `mobileNav` module has an `onclick` handler but lacks corresponding `onkeydown` handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot activate the toggle using standard keys.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `render` function in the `suggestionListRenderer` module injects HTML into the suggestion list using `innerHTML` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the new suggestions to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 as dynamic content changes are not announced.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module uses `setTimeout` to hide the overlay after a CSS transition but does not provide a user-controllable mechanism to pause or stop the auto-close. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) for users who need more time to interact with the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10140: Replace the `setTimeout` with a user-dismissible mechanism or ensure the timeout is longer than 20 seconds to comply with WCAG 2.2.1. Alternatively, remove the auto-close behavior for the overlay.

issuesFound.title: focus() not called on modal/dialog open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module opens the navigation drawer but does not programmatically move focus into the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as keyboard users are not directed to the new content.

issuesFound.title: focus() not returned to trigger on modal/dialog close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the `mobileNav` module closes the navigation drawer but does not return focus to the navigation toggle button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 as keyboard users lose their place in the focus order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10130: Add the following before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`:
```
if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
```

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field in the `newsletterForm` module lacks an `autocomplete` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) as browsers and assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the input's purpose, reducing usability for users who rely on autocomplete features.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13000: Add the following after `inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']);`:
```
_emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');
```

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle element (SELECTORS.navToggle) is bound to click events but lacks matching keydown handlers for Enter and Space. This prevents keyboard users from opening/closing the mobile menu, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle functions (open/close) change the visual state of the menu but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, leaving screen reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10815: Add after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`
```
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
```
    Line 10835: Add after `removeClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`
```
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, as screen readers cannot announce the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10845: Add after `debug('mobileNav.init: ready');`
```
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation state change (open/close) is not announced to screen readers via an ARIA live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages, leaving screen reader users unaware of the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10818: Add after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`
```
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
```
    Line 10838: Add after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`
```
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form validation function (_validateAll) marks fields as invalid by adding error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, leaving screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11405: Add after `errors.push({ field, message: result.firstError, label: cfg.label });`
```
setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
```

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation function (_renderResult) clears error messages and classes for valid fields but does not clear aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, leaving screen reader users with incorrect state information.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (SELECTORS.billingMonthly, SELECTORS.billingAnnual) are toggled visually but do not update aria-pressed. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, leaving screen reader users unaware of the selected billing period.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period toggle buttons do not have aria-pressed set on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value, as screen readers cannot announce the initial selected state.

issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links and social links have `text-decoration: none` and rely solely on color changes on hover to indicate interactivity. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive these as links (WCAG 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1513: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { text-decoration: underline; }
  Line 1523: .social-links a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple text elements use `--colour-text-secondary` (#aaaaaa) on a white background (`--colour-bg-page` #ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.8:1, which fails the minimum 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Hero image transitions missing reduced motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition that is not disabled in the prefers-reduced-motion media query (the existing query block is empty).
issuesFound.fix: Line 890: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Skip link transition missing reduced motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link has a transform transition that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: (add after .skip-link rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Product card transitions missing reduced motion support
issuesFound.explanation: The product-card class includes transform in its transition but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the lift animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1040: (add after .product-card rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which tightens character spacing and can reduce readability for users with visual impairments or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Primary button insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses background-color: #5588bb with color: #ffffff. The contrast ratio is approximately 4.12:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text at 12px font size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: .btn-primary { background-color: #004aaa; border-color: #004aaa; color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Skip link insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses background-color: #5588bb with color: #ffffff, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.12:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: background-color: #004aaa;

issuesFound.title: Danger button insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses background-color: #ee9999 with color: #ffffff. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 770: .btn-danger { background-color: #991b1b; border-color: #991b1b; color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without ensuring that links provide an alternative non-colour visual indicator (such as underline on hover) to distinguish them from surrounding text. Many link instances in the stylesheet (navigation, footer, social links) only change colour on hover, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 372: a { text-decoration: underline; } (or ensure all interactive links have underline or other non-colour indicator on hover/focus)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible but does not provide a visible replacement focus indicator such as box-shadow, border-color change, or an outline with a solid color. Keyboard users navigating to the mobile navigation toggle will not see where focus is located.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible but lacks a replacement visual indicator. Keyboard users navigating to the logo link will not see a focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible with no visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users will not see focus on the search submit button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible replacement. Keyboard users navigating account links will not see focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none and outline-offset but does not define a visible outline style or alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users navigating dropdown menu items will not see focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible without a visible replacement. Keyboard users navigating "Learn more" links in feature cards will not see focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative focus style. Keyboard users expanding FAQ items will not see focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible without a visible replacement. Keyboard users focusing the scrollable table region will not see a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible replacement. Keyboard users navigating footer links will not see focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible without a visible replacement. Keyboard users focusing brand links in the footer will not see focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible replacement. Keyboard users navigating social media links will not see focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons will be difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users may have difficulty checking/unchecking boxes.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility and may expose content that should be hidden.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The selector includes transform in its transition property and .btn:active uses transform: translateY(1px), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The selector includes transition on transform and hover state uses transform: perspective(...) rotateY(...) rotateX(...), but there is no reduced-motion override. The empty @media block at line 855 does not disable the transform.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The selector includes transition on transform and the :hover state uses transform: translateY(-2px), but there is no reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The selector includes transition on transform and the :hover state uses transform: translateY(-3px), but there is no reduced-motion override in the empty media block at line 1125.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The selector includes transition on transform and the :hover state uses transform: translateY(-2px), but there is no reduced-motion override in the empty media block at line 1225.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The selector includes transition on transform and the :hover state uses transform: translateY(-2px) and scale(1.01), but there is no reduced-motion override in the empty media block at line 1325.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or change to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: The link inherits text-decoration: none from the global a rule and only changes color on hover without adding an underline or other non-color visual indicator. Users with color vision deficiencies may not distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The link inherits text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover without adding an underline or border-bottom. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive the state change.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The link inherits text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover without adding an underline or other non-color indicator. Users with color vision deficiencies may not distinguish the link from surrounding text.

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline with no replacement visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the outline with no visible replacement. Users navigating by keyboard cannot see when the card link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing an alternative visible focus style. Keyboard users cannot see when the scrollable region has focus.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline with no visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when the accordion button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the brand link.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector removes the outline with no visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot perceive focus on footer links.

issuesFound.title: Social links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when social links have focus.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24×24 pixel minimum. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited precision.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24×24 pixel minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users may struggle to toggle the checkbox.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and normal dimensions, which keeps content fully visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern and may expose visual content meant only for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Hero image motion not reduced for prefers-reduced-motion
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img is empty, so users who prefer reduced motion still see the transform transition and hover animation. This violates WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: Product card motion not reduced for prefers-reduced-motion
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card class defines a transform transition but has no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the translateY movement. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and fails WCAG 1.4.12 text spacing requirements.

issuesFound.title: Input hint text colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint class uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa. Against the white or near-white background (var(--colour-bg-page) #ffffff or var(--colour-bg-subtle) #f8fafc), this provides a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter input hint colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section .input-hint uses rgba(255,255,255,0.65) (approximately #a6a6a6) against the brand primary background (#5588bb). This results in a contrast ratio below 4.5:1, making the hint text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Statistics label colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) against a semi-transparent white background over a blue gradient. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, well below the 4.5:1 required for AA compliance, making the labels illegible for many users.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight change for body text links. Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1.

fpTitles: Statistics label colour contrast insufficient

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers (SELECTORS.navDropdownTrigger) are toggled via _openInstance() and _closeInstance(), but these functions do not set aria-expanded to "true" or "false" on the trigger buttons. The register() function also fails to initialize aria-expanded="false" when setting up new dropdown instances. Assistive technology users cannot perceive the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown panels.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2178: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line 2193: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the start of the function
  Line 2238: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded via the open() function, the code adds CSS classes and animates the panel height but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the missing update in open() leaves screen reader users unaware that the item has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3490: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation markInvalid function not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function associates an error message with a field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input field. Screen readers cannot convey to users that the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1478: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation markValid function not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error association via aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Fields that were previously invalid continue to be announced as invalid by screen readers even after being corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1493: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` after the existing logic that updates aria-describedby

issuesFound.title: Product filter results changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function dynamically shows and hides product cards and updates the count badge, but these changes are not announced via a live region. When users apply filters, screen reader users receive no feedback about how many products match the selected criteria or that the grid content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3295: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`\${totalMatches} products found\`);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing amount changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), the _updatePlanCard() function updates the displayed price amounts via textContent changes, but these price changes are not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed that the price values have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Price updated to \${target} per \${period}\`);` after `plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-label', target);` in the _updatePlanCard function (inside the if (plan.priceDisplay) block)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when opening or closing panels. The setExpanded helper function exists in the codebase but is not utilized within these toggle functions, leaving screen readers unaware of the dropdown state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1764: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); Line 1780: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when items are dynamically opened or closed. While initialization sets the attribute, subsequent toggles via open() and close() functions fail to update it, causing screen readers to report stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2444: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); Line 2475: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS class but do not set aria-pressed to reflect the selected state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3008: Add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field element. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation clears an error state, the function removes aria-describedby references but fails to set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers may continue to report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1252: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); or Line 1252: Add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without activating a focus trap. The _trap variable is declared but never assigned to a createFocusTrap instance, allowing keyboard users to tab outside the drawer while it is visually open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates with new visible items and the count badge updates, but these dynamic content changes are not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that filtering has completed or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2862: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterAppliedCount || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/quarterly/annual), the price values update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or which period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2678: Add liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Switched to {period} billing').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, the trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to "false" to indicate that the menu is initially closed. Screen readers may not announce the correct initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1898: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown instances, the trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false". Screen readers may not convey the correct initial collapsed state of dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element is assigned role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox" during initialization, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially to indicate that the suggestion panel is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2960: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation function updates visual error states and aria-describedby references but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear aria-invalid on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey field validity states.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3838: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');
  Line 3843: removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no live region announcement informs screen reader users how many results are displayed or that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3125: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing dynamic content update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3540: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders a label element that receives an htmlFor prop but never applies it to the label element. This breaks the programmatic association between the label and the input field, preventing screen reader users from knowing which label describes which input.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator in FormGroup uses a bare asterisk character that is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "star" without context, which is confusing.

Since errorId is not provided, the ID does not exist in the DOM when an error occurs, breaking the association for screen reader users.

issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without providing the required aria-label prop. Since the button contains only an emoji icon (which is hidden from assistive technology), screen reader users hear no accessible name for this control.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class is broken — content is visible instead of hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible to sighted users. This is the opposite of the intended purpose. Screen-reader-only content (like skip links, hidden labels) will be visible on screen, breaking the visual design and potentially confusing users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 289-298: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-fast)), and .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px). Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this spatial animation, which can cause vestibular discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Skip-link transform transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes transition: transform var(--transition-base) and uses transform: translateY(-200%) for positioning. Users who prefer reduced motion will experience animation when the skip-link receives focus, which can cause vestibular discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 328: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Stat label has insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label rule sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (white at 30% opacity) on a gradient background from #5588bb to #bb88ee. issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query provides no override

issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state where closed items are correctly marked but opened items are not.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid via markInvalid(), the function only adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message but never sets aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not notified that the field contains an error.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field becomes valid, markValid() removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may retain aria-invalid="true" from a previous invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the trigger element does not receive an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. The ARIA state is not available when the page first loads.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands a FAQ item but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state where opening never updates the attribute.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message but never sets aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the aria-describedby reference but never clears aria-invalid. Fields that were previously marked invalid remain with aria-invalid="true" even after becoming valid.
